title: scrophularia aquatica var. parviflora
intro: Scrophularia aquatica var. parviflora, also known as Water Figwort, is a plant species that belongs to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory infections, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating coughs and sore throats, and supporting the immune system. Scrophularia aquatica var. parviflora can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
